gap_summary.rough_summary: EXTRACT(field FROM ts) - canonical SQL date-part extractor
Leftmost N characters (extfns has leftstr alias)
Current timestamp (SQLite has only current_timestamp keyword)
1-based position of substring (SQL POSITION operator); SQLite has instr but not position
Repeat string N times (extfns has replicate alias)
Rightmost N characters (extfns has rightstr alias)
Character count (alias of length in SQLite)
Current authenticated user (n/a for SQLite, but commonly stubbed)
Char from code point (SQLite has char only)
Subtract interval from date (chrono has date_add but not date_sub)
Difference in days between two dates (chrono has date_diff)
Decode bytea (PG variant; MySQL legacy crypto)
Human-readable byte size (humansize ext likely covers this)
Random in [0,1) - SQLite has random returning int
Geometry to WKB (postgis-bridge has st_asbinary as canonical)
Geometry to WKT (postgis-bridge has st_astext as canonical)
Add interval to timestamp (alias of date_add)
Truncate decimals (SQLite math has trunc; truncate is alias)
Add days/interval to date (MySQL-ism, alias of date_add)
Top-K most frequent values (covered: sketches ext)
Encode bytea (PG style) / string to bytes
Flatten nested array by one level
IPv6/IPv4 text to binary (covered: ipaddr likely)
Capitalize first letter of each word (extfns has proper)
Append to JSON array at path
Insert into JSON array at index
Advance and return next sequence value
SHA-2 family hash (256/384/512). SQLink has sha3; hashes-fast may already do this
Nth field after splitting by delimiter
Parse string to date with format (chrono has date_parse)
Concatenate strings with delimiter (sqlite has group_concat)
Parse string to numeric with format
Seconds since year zero or interval

new_extensions_proposed.name: stats
new_extensions_proposed.rationale: Aggregate/statistical functions are the single biggest cluster of missing surface (20+ in 3+ DBs, 6+ ubiquitous). SQLite ships only avg/sum/count/min/max/group_concat/total. PostgreSQL/MySQL/MariaDB/DuckDB/Snowflake/BigQuery all have the full SQL-standard set. We have hyperloglog and sketches for approximate, but no exact aggregates. A 'stats' extension delivering these as create_function_v2 aggregates would close a huge analytics gap with minimal LOC.

new_extensions_proposed.name: list
new_extensions_proposed.rationale: Array/list functions are documented across all 6 DBs (DuckDB has 80+, PG has 30, Snowflake/BigQuery 20+) but SQLink has zero general-purpose list/array surface. Pick a representation (JSON arrays via json1, or a real ARRAY type via a vtab) and build a Postgres/DuckDB-flavored array library. Especially valuable as the ML/vector world (vec0) increasingly works with embedding lists.

new_extensions_proposed.name: stdsql
new_extensions_proposed.rationale: SQL-standard scalar functions that every other DB ships. Many are trivial aliases or 5-line wrappers (e.g., string_agg=group_concat, if=iif, left=substr, char_length=length). Packaging them as a single 'stdsql' or 'compat' extension lets users write portable SQL without rewriting CASE/iif/substr idioms. Could also live as extensions to text-utils/core depending on architecture.

new_extensions_proposed.name: sys-compat
new_extensions_proposed.rationale: Session/identity functions are needed for portability of analytics tooling (BI tools, ORMs) that emit version()/current_user() probes. All return predictable constants or simple PRAGMA-derived values in SQLite. Trivial extension that increases compatibility-testing pass rate dramatically.

new_extensions_proposed.name: chrono (extend existing)
new_extensions_proposed.rationale: chrono today only has date_add/date_diff/date_format/date_parse/duration_*. 38 datetime functions appear in 3+ DBs and are missing; many are aliases of existing chrono functions (datediff -> date_diff, timestampadd -> date_add, str_to_date -> date_parse). Adding these as thin aliases plus a handful of new constructors (make_date, make_time, etc.) and extractors (year, month, day, ...) takes chrono from 'minimal' to 'PostgreSQL-class'.

new_extensions_proposed.name: json-sqlpath (or extend json1)
new_extensions_proposed.rationale: SQL/JSON path functions (SQL:2016 standard) are present in 3-5 of the 6 DBs. SQLite's json1 ships json_extract but not the SQL/JSON path family. Either extend json1 with these (preferred) or ship as a sibling. json_table as a vtab would be especially valuable for ELT pipelines.

notes:
Analysis covered 1711 SQLink scalar functions across 219 extensions, plus 172 SQLite native builtins (queried via PRAGMA function_list in this build, which includes the math + json + window functions). 2949 function entries were normalized across the 6 input inventories; 331 names appear in 2+ DBs and are absent from both SQLink and SQLite, 142 in 3+, 63 in 4+, 30 in 5+, 11 in all 6.

Methodology notes:
- Names were lowercased and matched as identifiers; aliases listed in each inventory were folded into the canonical name and also counted independently when used as a separate first-class name elsewhere.
- Window functions (cume_dist, dense_rank, first_value, lag, last_value, lead, nth_value, ntile, percent_rank, rank, row_number) appear in every inventory but are SQLite builtins (this build compiled with ENABLE_DBSTAT/JSON1/WINDOWFUNC); excluded from gaps.
- gen_random_uuid/uuid_v4/uuid_v7/UUID functions: SQLite now ships uuid/uuid_blob/uuid_str natively (per PRAGMA function_list); excluded.
- json_extract/json_object/json_array/json_set/json_remove/json_replace/json_insert/json_quote/json_valid/json_type/json_array_length/json_patch/json_group_array/json_group_object: all SQLite json1 builtins; excluded.

Inventory data-quality observations:
- The MariaDB inventory lists 'next value for' and 'previous value for' which are SQL keywords, not callable identifiers; filtered out by regex.
- PostgreSQL's docs include some odd entries like 'json' (one-arg cast) and 'text' (inet to text) that read as functions but are actually casts; included as functions but flagged as ambiguous.
- ClickHouse's 'isvalidascii' has many CamelCase variants normalized; some normalization may lose information about argument shape.
- Snowflake/BigQuery share many SQL standard functions but use very different naming for date arithmetic (Snowflake DATEADD/DATEDIFF vs BigQuery DATE_ADD/DATE_DIFF) - I treated DATEADD/DATEDIFF as the canonical and put BQ names in aliases.
- The SNOW_BQ inventory the previous phase produced collapses two engines; for cluster counts that's slightly optimistic toward 'BigQuery+Snowflake counts as one DB'. If we wanted strict per-engine counts we'd split.

Highest-value, lowest-effort wins (recommendations):
1. Add a 'stats' extension with the SQL-standard exact aggregates (stddev/variance family + covar + corr + percentile_cont/disc + mode + median + bit_and/or/xor + any_value + string_agg alias for group_concat). Roughly 25 aggregates, all expressible with running-sum or two-pass algorithms; ~600 LOC total.
2. Extend chrono with the missing 38 datetime functions. Most are 1-3 LOC aliases or extractors over existing julianday/strftime.
3. Add SQL/JSON path functions (json_value, json_query, json_exists, json_table) to json1. json_table as a vtab is the biggest LOC item; the scalars are wrappers over existing json_extract logic.
4. Add a small 'sys-compat' shim returning constants/PRAGMA values for current_user/session_user/database/version etc. - 50 LOC, huge compatibility-test win.
5. Add aliases in existing extensions: chr->char (text-utils), if->iif (core), datediff->date_diff (chrono), timestampadd->date_add (chrono), st_aswkb->st_asbinary (postgis-bridge), st_aswkt->st_astext (postgis-bridge), to_base64/from_base64->base64 (baseN or text-utils). These are pure aliases and could be auto-generated.

Items intentionally excluded from new-extension proposals:
- Sequence functions (nextval/currval/setval/lastval): 3+ DBs have them but they require persistent state (counter table); deserves its own design discussion before scaffolding an extension.
- BigQuery AEAD/KMS, Snowflake AI/Cortex/CATBOOST: vendor-specific or stateful; out of scope for portable extensions.
- ClickHouse hash zoo (xxhash, cityhash, farmhash, metrohash, sip, wy, murmur): individually low usage and partially covered by hashes-fast. If we wanted to expand we'd add to hashes-fast.
- H3/S2 spatial grids: SQLink already has h3 and s2 extensions; differences are naming (h3_to_cell vs h3_latlng_to_cell). Worth adding aliases.

What surprised me:
- 11 functions are in all 6 DBs and absent from SQLite + SQLink: ascii, extract, greatest, least, left, lpad, now, position, repeat, right, rpad. These are extremely low-hanging fruit. A 200-LOC PR adding them as `stdsql` would close a major portability gap and is independent of any architectural decision.
- Window functions are universal but already covered by SQLite, so the appearance of huge overlap there is illusory. After excluding SQLite builtins the picture is cleaner.
- Statistical aggregates are the single most ubiquitously-missing category. Investing in a proper 'stats' aggregate extension would be the biggest portability lift.
